steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: A noun phrase “one of many nonpresidents” appears before the blank, with a prepositional phrase “on US currency” following. The blank needs a word that modifies the noun and specifies which nonpresidents are referenced.

steps.title: Apply the infinitive phrase rule
steps.content:
An infinitive phrase correctly serves as a restrictive modifier of a noun. To appear expresses the meaning “that appear”: “nonpresidents to appear on US currency” is equivalent to “nonpresidents that appear on US currency.” Finite verbs (appears, had appeared, did appear) cannot function as noun modifiers in this position.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
